description: The lineage merge rule (element-wise MAX) is what keeps maxIterations sound across a merge node on a cycle. An each node cycles back into itself through a buffer (a merge node); the operation is a wildcard (always-more) mock, so only the per-lineage cap can stop the loop. The buffer must carry the each node's per-lineage iteration count across its flush without lowering it (the spec: a merge never lowers a count). If the merge instead took the element-wise MINIMUM (or otherwise dropped the count toward zero), the flushed event would re-enter the each node with a reset count, the cap would never be reached, and the loop would escape its bound and amplify without limit. The correct output is exactly three events; under the min-merge lineage-escape defect the output count blows up (running to the amplification backstop), so this fixture goes red against that defect. OG-EX-08 exercises the same rule but with a finite-response mock that stops the loop itself, so it cannot observe an unbounded escape; this wildcard variant can.

fixtures.name: Merge node on a cycle must not lower the iteration count (wildcard mock)
fixtures.description: items.next is a wildcard mock: every invocation emits {tick:true}. step (each, maxIterations 3) emits to output and also through gate (a buffer with limit 1, a merge node) and unwrap back into itself. The buffer flushes each single event, carrying step's per-lineage count forward unchanged (element-wise max over a one-element batch). step therefore opens exactly three invocations (counts 1, 2, 3) before the fourth arrival is dropped by the cap, so the output is three {tick:true} events. If the merge lowered the count instead of preserving it, step's count would reset on every pass and the cap would never bite, so the loop would amplify without bound.

graph.description: A cycle that routes an each node's output through a buffer (a merge node) and back into it, bounded only by maxIterations.
